title: [FIRRTL] IMDCE doesn't understand domain ports' users
body:
IMDCE needs to be taught about domain port users, otherwise it will delete them.  While I'm surprised this isn't a verifier crash, it still drops associations when their domain is dead.

Here's an example that may be a bit more complicated than necessary:

``` firrtl
FIRRTL version 6.0.0

circuit Dead:
  domain MyDomains:

  module Child:
    input d: Domain of MyDomains
    input e: Domain of MyDomains
    input x: UInt<1> domains [d]
    input clock: Clock domains [e]

    node n = unsafe_domain_cast(x, e)
    printf(clock, UInt<1>(1), "%d\n", n)

  public module Dead:
    input d: Domain of MyDomains
    input e: Domain of MyDomains
    input x : UInt<1> domains [d]
    input clock: Clock domains [e]

    inst c of Child
    connect c.x, x
    domain_define c.d = d
    domain_define c.e = e
    connect c.clock, clock
```

Building this with domain inference (`--domain-mode=infer-all`) and with and without optimizations (``, `--disable-opt`) yields MLIR that associates `Child>x` with domain `d` without optimizations but not when optimizations are enabled.
